channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: Tesla quarterly profits — 23 replies — 92.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Tesla quarterly profits

Open thread • Replies: 23 • Updated: 2026-04-05 14:35 UTC

OP

This shit will be making losses again in 1-2 years. And EV competition from other brands is only going to get stronger.

How is this a top 10 global company?

Early replies (first 3)

62076213
>>62076064
Never bet against a meme stock

62076218
>>62076213
Everyone right now is saying that shorting this is a fools game, so many people have been burned by this.
It will fall someday eventually. Then people will be saying it was an obvious short and you were foolish if you didn't take the opportunity.

62076380
i refuse to buy the sp500 anymore because it has this shit in the top10...ive been buying dgro instead and its been working lately

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62076384
>>62076064
The printer will tyrn backon, and chink EVs will be banned

62076385
>>62076064
>still talking about the cars
NGMI

62076389
>>62076385
I can't see the profit from the robots or Terafab on the picture.

62076524
>>62076064
im short tsla with 50k in puts target is 220 enjoy!

62076529
>>62076064
not a car company. mars. ai. future of humankind. visionary. already priced in

62076597
>>62076529
SAAAAR YOU SMELL LIKE SHIT SAAAAR

62076599
>>62076529
cope

62076684
>>62076064
Where is this chart from OP? Looks interesting

62076689
>>62076684
mobile version of this page:
>https://www.macrotrends.net/stocks/charts/TSLA/tesla/net-income

62076693
next quarterly report coming out sometime end of April btw and it's likely to be bad

62076695
Elon is going to have a melty

62076729
>>62076064
tesla is going to go to zero faster than anything you've ever seen once spacex IPO's. as soon as you see an IPO date, you need to be immediately going short or buying long dated way OTM puts.

62076736
>>62076064
He'll pivot to robots that automate wagie work, fail, and then pivot to Tesla as a Bitcoin holding company

62076739
>>62076729
My view is also that the SpaceX IPO is very bearish for Tesla stock. Elon fans will have to balance 2 stocks where to put their money in now, and SpaceX clearly has the better profits and future narrative.
Then again, I hear people claiming that Musk will use SpaceX IPO money to pump Tesla stock... 0_o idk, sounds like copium.

62078940
>>62076739
so, he's going to sell stock in one of his public companies to pump the other? sounds like zero-sum. not realistic at all. the problem is also that investment firms won't want double exposure to someone like elon. it's one thing to follow his cult-of-personality in Tesla or SpaceX alone. but the risk of volatility tied to both would not be palatable for most firms. SpaceX is the safer bet, Tesla's sales are dwindling, they're not delivering on any of the tech they've promised for the past 10+ years, they're running into regulatory issues for FSD. there's nothing after the SpaceX IPO that's bullish for Tesla save for SpaceX absorbing Tesla outright.

62078946
Youre onto something OP
You should buy OTM puts on tesla its basically free money

62078974
>>62076064
It is a growing company making huge investments into software and infrastructure that will change the world. They aren't trying to make big profits in the present. Don't buy a stock like this if you just want a comfy safe dividend.

62078980
>>62078940
The stock will pump itself when there are millions of Teslas on the road driving themselves and autonomous robots making everyone's lives easier and no other company can do what they are doing with AI. Trust the plan.

62079041
Don't know why that invest answers guy was so sure it would go up forever. Always was weird to me. There is only so much demand for shitty electric cars.

62079055
>>62076739
I like what SpaceX has done but many airliners much less a space company is a few crashes/failed launches from falling apart. Especially anything manned. I'm skeptical.

item.title: Renting 38% of net pay in VHCOL — 16 replies — 63.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
Renting 38% of net pay in VHCOL

Open thread • Replies: 16 • Updated: 2026-04-05 14:26 UTC

OP

Got a job paying ~12k net in VHCOL city. If I stay at the job, my income will steadily increase. It is impossible to find anything within decent commutable distance for less than 4k in that area that's not a complete run down shit hole or a 300sqft studio. I found a place for 4.6k that I really like but it will be 38% of my net pay (exc. utilities).

I know this goes against the usual PF advice, but I value my living space a lot and after working 70-80hrs the last thing I want is be depressed in some shitty apartment. On the other hand, I'm sacrificing so much of my time to give a ton of it away to the landlord. Thoughts?

Early replies (first 3)

62078197
bump

62078200
>>62078160
if your commute is over 1.25 hours then just do it. ive been in ur same shoes didnhalf commute half live next to work and ten years later now i dont regret renting nearby.

commute absolutely kills your soul

62078205
well, it's $4.6k/month down the drain. only you can decide if it's worth it

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62078208
God damn thats a lot. Just rent a shitbox. Burning 50k a year just for rent is killing your long term prosperity and will just delay retirement

62078211
Live in a shared 2 bedroom 1 bathroom house for less money. High rent eats your savings and commuting eats your time which is worse. If you are young then go for 4 roommates 1 bathroom and have fun partying with your roommates.
> t. lived in a shared 2 bed 1 bath house for years until roommate got tired of listening to me jerkin it and started slamming doors, then i moved out to a ~400sf studio

62078215
What job / industry?

62078265
>>62078160
Seems to be a lot of room between supreme luxury penthouse and shitbox. Landlords are generally amenable to you fixing things up so just focus on the area. The neighbors and that.

62078277
>working 70-80hrs
You're a wagie who will barely be home except to sleep. Just rent a cheap room to sleep in.

62078295
>>62078160
SF? NYC? The price tag is worth it if you have both leisure time and disposable income to enjoy them.

Considering the “70-80” hour work weeks, that’s unsustainable. If you do that more than once a year, you’ll eventually want to throw yourself off the balcony of the nicest penthouse $12k could afford. Your goal in life with a salary is to work as little as possible.

Yes, any commute is terrible. If you’re taking public transit in those two cities, it’s doable to live in an affordable area. I credit great public transit for giving me time to study and let my mind wander early in my career as a tech professional. An extra hour in your work day isn’t going to be worse than having no savings.

Here’s my warning - coastal high pressure industries like tech, law, and finance are churn and burn. They promise dreams, drain the youth and vitality, then kick the once-eager husks to the curb once a pulse of resistance is felt. I have seen many people fall from grace and unsuccessfully cope with drugs, women, flashy cars, nice apartments, and all of those markers of a successful life. Save money to prepare for layoffs and your next move. Don’t be a sucker and spend your life in a VHCOL without getting all you can.

62078404
>>62078160
Retard.

62078728
My counter to all of this is what's the point of making money if you aren't spending it? Sure, don't be a dumbfuck who doesn't save anything and wastes money but having a good place to live is not wasting money for me. There's also a social capital element of having a nice place where you can invite people. I know I will be successful and make more money and I feel like if anything spending more here will encourage me to find additional ways to make more.

62078770
>>62078160
If you find a partner who isn't a deadbeat then she could potentially pay half of the rent. Have that thought ever crossed your mind? If she is a catch maybe you will have to settle for paying 70% of the rent, still a major discount plus a wet hole bonus.

62078873
>>62078770
in the current stage of my life i'd rather fuck foids before I move to a city where most women don't have fucked up liberal values

62079016
>>62078873
Why do you care about their values if they pay rent and give access to their wet hole? The only problems are minor stuff like snoring and sharing a bed in general, and having to occasionally wait on her when you need the bathroom. Just find one that doesn't do any drugs, including alcohol and nicotine. There are plenty of these.

62079032
>>62078160
If youre White you need to leave the city immediately. If youre not, then I guess just continue enjoying your destruction of Western civilization while it still lasts. This is financial advice

62079040
>>62078295
^ Best advice in the thread.

item.title: Apparently a lot of boomers visit sites like >Bloomberg >Financial Times etc What for? — 12 replies — 48.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Apparently a lot of boomers visit sites like >Bloomberg >Financial Times etc What for?

Open thread • Replies: 12 • Updated: 2026-04-05 14:37 UTC

OP

Apparently a lot of boomers visit sites like
>Bloomberg
>Financial Times
etc
What for? What's the value there? Do they read some article saying "buy banan, here's my analysis why" and then they buy banan? They read shill articles and listen to shill advice? That's it?
What's the use case for such sites from an investors POV?

Early replies (first 3)

62078584
>1980s
News is actually useful
>2020s
We know *ews lie about everythign to trick people into bagholding shit while they make off with the loot.

62078598
>>62078558
They just like to read. Even if the article is about how vegan food restaurants causes gentrification and are therefore bad for the environment.

62078668
in ancient times people would read about current events and watch tv and then go on to discuss it with other people that had done the same

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62078676
Just staying up to date on current events without spending 14 hours of screen time scrolling x or tiktok

62078678
>>62078558
boomers think reading literal propaganda is what educated refined citizens do
also they thinks its not propaganda

62078687
>>62078584
>>1980s
>News is actually useful
My brother, it's nearly a hundred years since Citizen Kane was made.

62078690
>>62078558
As opposed to shitcoin discord zoomer fags?

62078745
>>62078558
Are you autistic by any chance? Autists have a hard time accepting that everybody isn't a clone of themselves, and that other people might think differently from themselves.
>What for?
Keeping an eye on the goings-on in the business world
>What's the value there?
Finding out about the goings-on in the business world
>Do they read some article saying "buy banan, here's my analysis why" and then they buy banan?
No. Unless Cramer says "buy banan" then they chuckle a bit, and if they currently own banan then they sell their banan.
>They read shill articles and listen to shill advice?
No, they keep an eye on the goings-on in the business world.
>That's it?
Yes, that's literally it. Some people prefer Bloomberg &c to the endless bots and jeets here on /biz/. Some people have no interest in shitcoins or bitcoins or whatever bag you're trying to shill people into buying.

62078794
>>62078678
Well they were "taught" just that and that's what all the boomer normies did on a superficial level

62078802
>>62078668
Yeah boomers love to consume propaganda / paid commercial articles and then gossip about it. Not sure what's the value in doing that tho.

62079052
>>62078802
Yeah zoomers love to consume shillbot / pajeet propaganda posts and then gossip about it. Not sure what's the value in doing that tho.

62079057
>>62078794
Let's be real they are victims of propaganda but didn't get redpilled enough to say hey this is bullshit. They kinda ate it up.

item.title: Financially Fucked By My Father — 12 replies — 48.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Financially Fucked By My Father

Open thread • Replies: 12 • Updated: 2026-04-05 14:31 UTC

OP

My dad has no life insurance. Still owes $75k on the mortgage with 8 years to go. He only has $5k in the bank and $5k in his 401k at 63 years old.

He doesn't do ANY maintenance on the house. The basement hasn't had a dehumidifier in it for 7 years and it's full of rat shit and probably mold. The roof is overdue by 2 years and it'll cost about $20k for a new roof. Water collects around the foundation of the house when it rains and he doesn't do anything about it. The water filters need to be changed twice a month, but he only does it once a month, so there's rust in both bathrooms. The master bathroom bathtub drain leaks into the basement and he hasn't fixed it in 4 years, so he just showers in the other bathroom. The kitchen sink leaks water. The fridge is brown from dirt and grime. The stove is covered in dirt and grime. The microwave above the stove is broken, so he has a separate microwave on the counter. The carpet is 20 years old and destroyed. The laminate flooring is peeling away. The window sills are bubbled and peeled from water damage. The furnace is 20 years old. The garage has no electricity running to it and the garage door is broken. He doesn't even fix that when he used to install and repair garage doors as a job for 30 years. There are cobwebs in the living areas. The porches are falling apart and it's a tripping hazard every time you walk up the stairs.

All he does is get drunk, jackoff to porn, blast music and mow the lawn.

Having a parent that is lazy, financially retarded and unhygienic will screw you over when it comes to owning and maintaining a home. I refuse to put $100k into the house to make it enjoyable to live in, especially when he's still living there.

My mom is awesome though and she lives with me.

Are you in the same situation, or did you get lucky enough to have intelligent, hardworking parents that actually care about your future finances?

Early replies (first 3)

62078756
>>62078722
so sound like a faggot

62078808
>insure house alot
>fire
>profit

62078820
>>62078722
Not really seeing how your dad's financial issues affect you at all though, much less how you are "financially fucked" by this.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62078843
Get off your lazy pasty cracka ass and do it yo goddamn self. Your dad is probably like 70

62078854
>All he does is get drunk, jackoff to porn, blast music and mow the lawn.

Based?

62078870
>>62078820
>what is generational wealth
Classic retard.

62078891
>>62078870
Thank you. I didn't feel like replying to something that stupid.

62078925
my grandad bought my dad a business which he ran into the ground. then he sold it when he was 50 and has been 'retired' since - he's 80 now.

62078942
>>62078925
actually i just remembered, he actually ran the first business into the ground, sold it, then started a new one with the money and then ran the second one into the ground and then 'retired'
generational run t bh

62078968
>>62078870
Your dad doesn't owe you generational wealth though. His failure to gift you millions of dollars does not in any way constitute "fucking you over financially". It is his house, to do with as he pleases, and until and unless he wills it to you, it is of no business to you, you entitled little shit.

62079025
>>62078968
So true king, fuck family they dont deserve shit from you.

62079045
>>62079025
Not once they hit 18 they don't. Also I don't know if you were intending to come across like a whiny little bitch, but you did an excellent job of it.

item.title: What price will TQQQ be slurpable again? — 6 replies — 20.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
What price will TQQQ be slurpable again?

Open thread • Replies: 6 • Updated: 2026-04-05 14:23 UTC

OP

What price will TQQQ be slurpable again?

Early replies (first 3)

62078302
above $55 or in 10 years time, there is no downside target

62078334
>>62078301
assuming the long term uptrend on QQQ continues, very low 20s or thereabouts

62078339
>>62078301
keep waiting. this year is going to be fucked up

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62078456
elon gonna send that shit straight down to hell with spacex ipo

62078570
I often think for the past 3 year what a stupid faggot I was for not buying in summer 2022 when shit was low, instead I was still panic selling the bottom.

62079036
>>62078570
Maybe we'll get another chance anon

item.title: Is it worth it to pay the penalty to cash out my 401k and dca into MSTR? — 3 replies — 10.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
Is it worth it to pay the penalty to cash out my 401k and dca into MSTR?

Open thread • Replies: 3 • Updated: 2026-04-05 14:23 UTC

OP

Is it worth it to pay the penalty to cash out my 401k and dca into MSTR?

Early replies (first 3)

62079019
Don't ever touch your 401k retard thats fake money that you dont have.

62079034
>>62079012
>Is it worth it to pay the penalty to cash out my 401k and [do something else with it]
Almost never. Unless you've got some insanely retarded debt with ultra high interest rates or a life or death medical emergency, you have to leave it in the boomerk. It's frustrating, but think of it as the cost of the free money you got from your company's match.

62079038
>>62079012
>MSTR
Cash it out and go all in on 100% physical silver.
This is financial advice.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

No additional replies in the latest window.

item.title: Polkadot - First halving — 247 replies — 121.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
Polkadot - First halving

Open thread • Replies: 247 • Updated: 2026-04-05 12:38 UTC

OP

https://polkadothalving.com/ 10 more days. https://www.parity.io/blog/refining-polkadots-economic-architecture-issuance-DOT-DAP-and-network-adjustments
https://x.com/Polkadot/status/2028482336463945765
"
Polkadot’s economic upgrade begins rolling out in 10 days.

Enhanced tokenomics increases DOT scarcity and introduces new governance and staking mechanisms.

▸ DOT supply capped at 2.1B
▸ Emissions cut 53.6%
▸ Unbonding from 28 days to 24-48 hours
"

Early replies (first 3)

61933757
Guys?

61933779
>>61932834
10000$ @ 6.5
i dont sell my dotty

61936705
Guys?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62056642
>>62056623
What do you mean "How do I buy this shit"? Bro, if this comes of as mean, it's not meant as mean and I'm sorry if it sounds mean. If you can't figure out how to buy something yourself then please just don't buy it at all. Make yourself a favor

>>62056631
You do realize that this won't be true forever? And that certain projects do decouple from BTC when they're ripe for it.

62056686
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=gg4P23PirgU

Oliver makes a really good point here where he tries to point out that like 4 years ago L2s could just exist and people would use them and NOW people are demanding that the L2s upgrade and improve in order to still be considered useful. At this point I would like to add an additional thing. The thing that people want from major L2s (like Arbitrum and AVAX and other stuff like that) is what DOT L2s already have. The major L2s can't compete with DOT because they're behind and once JAM hits they'll be even more behind. In the time DOT gets JAM the L2s won't even have what DOT has RIGHT NOW. DOT has shared security, it has built-in native SAFE bridging (that's not even a bridge, it's a XCM, it uses HRMP channels), because of the shared security it DOES NOT fragment liquidity, you can use the functionality of ANOTHER chain on DOT (so of other DOT chains and other chains in general that aren't on DOT). Once people realize that they can EASILY build on DOT while every other chains LIMITS them in some shape or form they'll start flocking to DOT. Every other chain has a ton of problems that DOT simply DOES NOT therefore DOT is technologically speaking superior. Sooner or later the developers will catch onto it and start developing on DOT since the other chains have limited capabilities. You can only develop humdrum applications on ETH, no innovation will ever come out of ETH ever again. ETH is obselete and unreconcilable. Sure it'll stay relevant for a few more year but how much longer can it stand the test of time? BTC is windows 1.0, ETH is windows 98, DOT is Windows 12 lol (Yes, not Windows 11, it's windows 12).

62056998
>>62056631
The game is changing. This bear market will prove it

62057110
>>62056998
>The game is changing.
What do you see anon? Help us understand what you see when you say "The game is changing". And yes, I do agree that stuff will shift from stupidity back to usability. So, meme coins were the LAST DROP. Enough is ENOUGH. We need to go back to our roots and invest into stuff that actually makes sense and is GROUND BREAKING. If we don't pivot in this liminal time then the industry as a whole will be finished for like 10-20 years UNTIL we pivot there is no way forward. We need to REALIZE that BTC is crap, ETH is crap, SOL is crap, USDT is centralized shit, XRP is useless, TRX is a cult run piece of shit, lol BCH is in the top 10 WTF, DOGE is crap, BNB is fucking crap. It's all just useless junk. Literally the whole of top 10 is fucking garbage that's unusable. It's literally all Windows 1 and Windows 98 type of coins while we already have the Vishnu of the mindchain, the Windows 12 of blockchains, the collectibe 1/9 Lamborghini Veneno Roadster of the blockchain, the fucking Rolls-Royce of the blockchain industry, and we're still stuck with the fucking Windows 1 and Windows 98 of the blockchain industry. Guys, it's time for FUCKING CHANGE. Either we pivot to the 3rd generation blockchains or the whole sector will GO UNDER for a decade or two. Do you want to suck cock for 10 to 20 years? Or do you just market sell your garbage gen 1 and gen 2 tokens/coins and move on with your life and buy the actual technical innovation? It's choice time and it's GOOD to know that any day you can change your whole fucking life. If you want the world to change then the first step to changing the world is to CHANGE YOUR FUCKING SELF. You only need to 1. WANT THE CHANGE and 2. BELIEVE YOU CAN CHANGE (Self-efficacy). Those are the 2 most important principles of BEHAVIOURAL CHANGE. Change your behaviour and with that you'll change the world. Sure it'll take time and effort but hey, even wallowing in your misery, being depressed takes time and effort.

62058962
>>62056631
Algorand is pumping while Bitcoin is dumping because it is quantum secure. Nigger

62059225
DOT will reach the $1.60 range before this week ends

62059268
>>62057110
See was that so hard on being influential?

62060540
>>62059268
The thing is, that "influenced" you... Doesn't mean it influenced the other people. Everyone is a unique little program and therefore needs unique inputs in order to produce the desired output. One man's trash is another man's treasure

>>62058962
Nagger

62060621
100$ end of the FUCKING bull run. Here's some influential and intellectual insights

62060648
>>62060540
How do you know. Besides the intent of you making this thread was to educate/shill/ influence the mass retards on this site, don't try to convince me otherwise

62062169
>>62060648
>How do you know.
What do you mean by that? How do I know what?

62062500
>>62060648
How I know that people are unique little programs and that I need to input unique inputs to get the desired outputs? Is that what you're asking or?

62062550
Let me "influence" you young Vishnus of the MINDchain. Listen to one of our spiritual leaders Oliver Talé-Yaźdi how he talks about the Vishnu mindchain. Join our cult *kmh* *kmh* web3 organization where we talk about the new world order.

https://x.com/Polkadot/status/2039256751346700502

62065568
100$ end of MIND RUN

62068446
12 more hours and the thread will be alive for 30 days and that's when Gavin Wood gives me 11.5k DOT for my "influencing" of your young Vishnus of the mindchain.

62068604
i buy this shit at 6.6$ or -75% from ATH
NOW I M AT -90% DOWN

62068658
>>62068604
yeah the decline of this shit has been remarkable. and it still somehow has a $2 billion market cap.

62069192
>>62068658
im very disappointed

62069282
>>62069192
Good. Now sell so I can GIGA short it some more while shilling it on 4chan. You activated my trap card anon "Backstabbing shill" (Effect: Convince people DOT is THE SOLUTION while having a giga short open at the same time).

62071796
100$ end of bull run. And it's official, the thread has stayed alive for 30 days and therefore Gavin Wood will pay me 11.5k$. That will feed Mumbai for literally a whole year.

Thank you Vishnu

62072073
>>62071796
You're welcome.

Don't I get a little something

62073144
>>62072073
But of course Panjali, your are a citizen of Mumbai city, the city of DOT. Every citizen gets paid. We all get feed and loved by our kind lord Gavin Wood. He is the underworld CP kingpin. He sells Cheese Pizza and then redistributes the funds among DOT holders. Everyone in Mumbai is eating and licking Cheese Pizza. I also like to put some cream onto my Cheese Pizza and then lick it off.

Best regard from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar

62075463
100$ end of bull run

Best regard from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar

62077075
Every day after the 30th day I get 150 DOT as a bonus. If we keep the thread alive for 30 more days I'll get 30 * 150 = 4500 more DOT. Please help contribute so my village of Mumbai gets feed.

62078039
>>62077075
You are not in India

62078216
>>62078039
This is my hand >>62008295 young Vishnu. This was taken directly from Mumbai, the city of DOT.

Best regard from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar

62078593
Dont selling my dotty anymore

62078595

62078631

62078789

item.title: >Setting up the next TACO for the market pump AGAIN. — 26 replies — 60.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
>Setting up the next TACO for the market pump AGAIN.

Open thread • Replies: 26 • Updated: 2026-04-05 14:15 UTC

OP

>Setting up the next TACO for the market pump AGAIN.
>This will still have an impact on markets
It's all so tiresome

Early replies (first 3)

62076909
>>62076906
but... what if... he doesnt taco this time........

62076912
>>62076906
how is this setting up the next TACO?

62076916
>>62076906
he's right though. The Scrum Masters are in trouble, so we need an emergency TACO and bailout.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62076917
>>62076912
Because he'll make up some bullshit about how Iran and the US made amazing progress with peace talks. He did the exact same thing two weeks ago on monday

62076919
>Gives 10 days for his insiders to accumulate monstrous shorts
>Actually bombs Iran to the stone age
I would not be surprised.

62076920
>>62076906
>remember when
The funny thing is nobody fucking remembered that lmao because he made up 10 more lies since then

62076922
>>62076906
Wake me up when they start carpet bombing Shiraz.

62076980
>>62076916
scrum master is some borat shit fakest job I ever heard of lmao

62077003
>>62076917
Iran has been getting pummeled all weeked. You retards act like nothing is being done.

62077004
>>62076906
Iran isn't gonna taco though. Why should they when they're winning. Keep it up until Israel is wrecked then the real run can begin.

62077041
>>62077003
>just 2 more weeks of bombing and they will give up and the one day special military operation will conclude

62077042
>>62076906
>hand over Gilgamesh and the staffs of the five wizards
>you have ten days, excluding banks holidays, except Martin Luther King day

62077047
>>62077003

62077102
>>62076906

>crypto didnt even blink
Yeah, bottom is in!

62077224
>>62076906
I voted for this

62077248
>>62076906
everyone should remember that no matter what, you are not going to outsmart the jews in this market

62077597
>>62077370

62077711
>>62077248
jews are not smart they just imagine it to be
>>62077370
>>62077597
kek

62078193
>>62076906
He is calling himself God now, aw hell nah. Let every red state turn into a Pride Parade with furfags and gays in latex gimpsuits.

62078275
>>62076912
dying

62078370
fuck off moralfags

62078379
this guy is such a faggot, i want to short sooo badly, but the algos pump the charts 3-5% every time this idiot backs down.

62078435
>>62076906
> Glory be to GOD!
wtf?

62078979
>>62078435

62079026

item.title: We need to talk. And I mean a serious conversation. What if this is the bottom of the bear market? With all the blatant… — 14 replies — 38.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
We need to talk. And I mean a serious conversation. What if this is the bottom of the bear market? With all the blatant…

Open thread • Replies: 14 • Updated: 2026-04-05 14:19 UTC

OP

We need to talk. And I mean a serious conversation.

What if this is the bottom of the bear market? With all the blatant manipulation, the threats, and the current mess, it feels like BTC just doesn’t want to go below 65–68k. We’re even above where we were before the war.

Curious to hear your thoughts.

Early replies (first 3)

62078491
>>62078487
don't be retarded. BTC follows the broader equities market, which is about to eat major shit.we could see BTC $40k within a couple months imo.

62078503
>>62078491
Crypto dumps first and pumps first. Short of a nuclear escalation I don’t see how things could realistically deteriorate further. Leverage has already been flushed

62078507
>>62078487
How can i have a serious conversation with that young gentleman as your webm?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62078508
>>62078491
>BTC $40k
I would cream my pants for a bitcoin that cheap

62078512
>>62078508
Because your ego tricks you into believing that you can accurately predict that it will go up again

62078532
>>62078512
I can. It's not my ego, I'm just right.

62078778
>>62078532
ego nigger

62078835
>>62078487
MOOOOOOOOODDDDDDDSSSSSSSS

62078978
>>62078487
Get this 17 year old off the board

62079001
>>62078491
It has sustained ridiculous sell pressure to still be at 60 something
I'm not sure it's going down a lot more anymore

62079020
>>62078487
one more flush

62079023
>>62078508
You'll be too much of a pussy to buy when it actually gets there

62079027
>>62078487
there is no serious conversation on this board
just post slampigs instead

62079031
>>62079027
This to be desu

item.title: Tom Zschach is Stepping down as CIO of Swift — 29 replies — 36.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Tom Zschach is Stepping down as CIO of Swift

Open thread • Replies: 29 • Updated: 2026-04-05 13:52 UTC

OP

Is he team Ripple or team Chainlink?

Early replies (first 3)

62070423
>>62070351
He hates Ripple and is pretty open about it. What's he moving on to?

62070569
>>62070423
hes moving to chainlink as their chief innovation officer lol

62070688
>>62070351
>>62070423
>>62070569
fake news
him stepping down would be hyper bullish for xrp because he has been spending a lot of time keyboard battling schizos
maybe they fired him?
just seems fake though I can't find anything

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62070697
>>62070688
wtf it's real lol
https://www.linkedin.com/posts/tomzschach_ive-spent-the-last-six-years-as-chief-innovation-activity-7445477170447110145-bukf
didn't realize it was stinkedin
swift is either donezo or they are going to use xrp kek what a timeline

62070709
>>62070688
>>62070697
He probably was forced to step down when every x.com post from an organization that handles cross border payment settlements was getting flooded by apu avatars asking about some stupid digital coin.

62071081
>>62070351
Crypto is really becoming depressing.
The talent bleed to AI is insane.

62071203
>>62070688
you're right he's actually joining chainlink to be sergays fucktoy. I don't blame him, he probably holds a lot and any link holder would get their ass pumped full of his big mac sauce if it meant pumping the price past ATH kek

62071238
>>62070423
>>62070688
How is this bullish for xrp?

62071622
>>62071238
Because they won

62071746
>>62071622
At what

62071869
>>62071746
They don't really understand anything so they just cope and seethe at anyone who points out the basic facts as to why xrp is objectively useless. Xrpaypigs are the dumbest cunts in crypto lol

62071875
>>62070688
>>62071238
>bullish for xrp
BOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM

62071884
Kek

62071954
>>62070351
I think he's team unemployment

62073155
>Now That XRP is Dead, What’s Next? Swift Executive Calls Ripple a Dead Chain Walking
>sep 2 2025
https://99bitcoins.com/news/altcoins/now-that-xrp-is-dead-whats-next-swift-executive-calls-ripple-a-dead-chain-walking/

Got fired out of SWIFT , 4 3 2026, 6 months later.
Another boomer who was blocking crypto and xrp got removed.

62073177
>>62073155
>boomer who was blocking crypto
He wasn't blocking crypto at all, the exact opposite.

He just saw xrp for what it was: stupid and useless.

62073659
anyone want to bet they don't even replace him at swift?

>chief innovation officer

LOL. sounds like a title you make up to hire someone's nephew

all this dude did was post online all day

62073668
Good riddance. Now Ripple can swiftly take over.

62073718
>>62070351
I heard pic related is going to be the next chief innovation officer

62074451
>>62073718
obviously this won't happen but it is an interesting hypothetical, what would we do if sergey left link to work at swift, the us gov, or one of the major banks? obviously the code within link would stay the same but would the culture still revolve around link or would people move on and just care about what serg is doing?

62074524
>>62074451
i don't think that's a problem you have to worry about stinkie. why would those esteemed institutions hire a known russian scammer

62074590
>>62074524
the same reason that the trump admin is letting a known russian scammer in the white house

62076260
>>62074590
Ikr? security was a mess at that event

62076265
This is very good news for XRP

62077595
>>62070351
Called it. He watched the DLT evolution incubate and come and develop and built out and now finally crystalize in final products used at large with plugins to 10s of thousands of banks and all he could come up with was GPI as a way to very moderately modernize fax machine era messaging tech and CCIP which is slow and expensive as fuck and injects middleman jew friction into services other products don't and presumes the reliance on consortium trust in a tech era that is automating the benefits the trust gave to insulate from bad actors, literally rendering them obsolete increasingly through time. It is AMAZING how much he missed the ball and how much weird specific communities still clung to him because he was /their guy/. He expected the Warrenite financialist cartel to triumph legally over crypto and leave it illegal or regulated into unusability and massively lost and made ZERO plans for alternative cases or backups. Now he is either being fired for doing WAY too little WAY too late or jumping ship because he realizes or always knew this and is looking to benefit elsewhere. He literally did what the best possible case would be for someone who hired him to make sure SWIFT missed the ball and failed and was left in the technologic smart dust. He is either the biggest idiot boomer in tech possibly literally in industrial history or he is a snake in the grass looking to cash in on a pre-arranged hire. Either way SWIFT is COOKED and it is EXTREMELY awesome to see because they are a corporatist hell organization.

62078616
>>62077595
This

62078702
>>62077595
So are the 11,500 FI's that own, operate and use SWIFT cooked?

62078729
>>62074451
Do billionaires often leave their own company to go work in a bank?

62078994
>>62078729
Yes billionaires leave their own company all the time and a handful of time they even get fired even though they are the reason for all the success.
Steve Jobs got fired at Apple
Martin Eberhard got fired from Tesla
Sam Altman got fired from OpenAI
If sergey feels as though chainlinks mission is complete I could very much see him getting hired by a monopoly to ensure they are the company to best use chainlinks services and make patents for their products as he is the true innovator and has all the ideas. Think about all the examples sergey has mentioned previously and then goes "well someone will build it" what if he wants to be the one building the actual products rather than the plumber. Plumbing gets boring after a while.

item.title: Going to Pattaya after being laid off? — 61 replies — 30.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
Going to Pattaya after being laid off?

Open thread • Replies: 61 • Updated: 2026-04-05 12:41 UTC

OP

33
House fully paid
Will be terminated in July 2026
120K in savings
No career (only issue here)

After 5 years being a good goy i got laid off and my contract will stop in July 2026

Should I allow myself 2-3 months in Pattaya to relax or should I "careermax" trying to get a new job immediately?

Resume gap I don't really care because a friend can say i work for his company during this time

im a junior cloud engineer (yes at 33 lol) and would like to get a certification like AZ-104 to be able to be hired as a Cloud engineer or Senior Cloud engineer when i come back home after that

What do you think?

PS: My gf is being very annoying about that but Im ready to go without her if she keeps nagging, FYI she is 100% remote and Pattaya is a comfy place if you stay away from naughty areas

Early replies (first 3)

62070612
>>62070405
https://youtu.be/sbcZzT8CExA

62070641
>>62070612
Yes this town

62070857
>>62070405
>Pattaya is a comfy place
no, its gross

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62075633
>>62070405
>PS: My gf is being very annoying about that but Im ready to go without her if she keeps nagging, FYI she is 100% remote and Pattaya is a comfy place if you stay away from naughty areas

>My gf is being very annoying
they all are
>ready to go without her
you're making the mistake I made in threatening to move without EX gf's full support. thought I could spearhead an expedition, if you will. No. everything will fall apart.
>she is 100% remote
she's not gonna do it even if work allows her and it'll save her a million bucks on rent per minute

62075687
>>62072028
no, the rural villagers are so poor they send their daughters into the city to be prostitutes

62075985
>>62070405
>My gf
Why would you go live in the gooner capital with a western foid in tow? Either ditch her or go somewhere else like Da Nang or Ho Chi Minh.

62076193
>>62075459
>if you go to pattaya for 2 months you will end up alone at 55

62076204
When oil hits 200 a barrel, SEA will be hit the hardest. I can't even imagine retiring in a literal pro town with girls that have bodies of 12 year old boys and have taken a mile of Indian cock. Fucking pathetic.

62076290
>>62076204
>mile of Indian cock.
3 inch dicks can't ruin vaginas Ramjeetdastinkamiya.

62076296
Try to get a job while you are still employed. The best way to get a new job is to have a job. Not being employed gives HR the ick.

62076309
>>62070405
I know what kind of man you are

62076421
>>62076309
You cant stop me from enjoying tight Thai teens you fuckin dork

62077483
>>62070405

Why aren't you moving there? I'm in a similar situation, but with no GF. Get DTV visa, work from there, live like a king. I'm a neet, so not brave enough to be a freelancer.

62077491
>>62072511

>creampies
>raw

Are STDs another jewish psyop?

62077502
>>62072697
Why tf would you go to Pattaya of all places then you retard?

62077556
>>62073426
>>62073426
X2

62077669
>>62077491
Yes and no. Its virtually impossible to get aids if you're a straight male. Even if you fuck a girl with aids unless you do anal and have abrasions on your dick its very unlikely you will get it. Oral herpes is another matter if you fuck around enough, not even with pros but just any women you are basically guaranteed to get it at some point but its also kind of a nothing burger its an occasional inconvenience at most so long as you are relatively healthy. Most of the other stuff is just bacterial infections that can be quickly and permanently cured with antibiotics and prevented entirely just by pissing after sex and washing your dick not to mention avoiding grody bitches.

62077688
Youre gonna die of food poisoning from a gutter oil food cart OP

62077697
>>62070405
>Pattaya is a comfy place if you stay away from naughty areas
Sex tourism is literally the only reason anyone would ever goes to Pattaya. If you want a relaxing beach holiday around regular people there are a ton of other options in SEA

62077700
I went to Thailand twice in my early 30s. Bangkok and Pattaya. I recommend going once, the first time was legendary, the second time it didn't really do much for me anymore at it was just sad.

62077827
>>62077669
this guy is a complete fucking moron outside of his hiv dismissal, which is somewhat accurate, ignore him. All bacterial STDs are approaching anti-biotic resistant status and some have no visible/noticeable symptoms, but can cause you serious health problems long-term.

Any hooker who lets you fuck her raw, lets most of her clients fuck her raw, and the almost all of the nastiest, filthiest subhuman males alive, pay for hookers, so do plenty of normal guys sure, but just keep that in mind.

62078089
>>62077827
It's like how you always hear of person 1837383 winning the lottery but never all the people that played their whole lives but didn't wint.

You don't hear of persons 1-1837382 getting terrible STDs because, instead of posting on 4chan, they're now dealing with their STD

62078268
>>62070405
Get another job now and have 3+ kids you fool

62078318
>>62070405
>PS: My gf is being very annoying about that but Im ready to go without her if she keeps nagging,
WTF WTF WTF

You're planning to take your GIRLFRIEND to PATTAYA???

Are you going to whore her out to pay for your stay, or does she really love threesomes with filthy Thai prostitutes?

62078320
>>62074237
>It's like beastiatility to me.
There's a pretty big underground bestiality scene in Thailand if you do some looking. Anything from plain old "watch the hooker get fucked by her dog" to k9 prostitution.

62078325
>>62076204
>and have taken a mile of Indian cock
Most of the hookers refuse to go with Indians. It's hilarious watching Sukdeep and Ranjeet grab at hookers on the street and get rejected. "No, Sukdeep, I no go wit you, you too rapey."

There is even a special D E S I G N A T E D street that is the ONLY one with hookers who cater to Indians. Even there, they won't go back to a hotel with an Indian because they know he'll have fifty of his friends waiting down the hallway to show up as soon as she gets inside the room, and they'll all expect to take turns on her without paying her anything. Nope, Sukdeep has to rent a room in the hooker's brothel and stay with her there.

Doesn't really matter, though. Pattaya has been ruined by the poos. They shit all over the beach, and they sleep on the beach too (right on top of piles of buried shit) because they can't afford hotel rooms.

62078523
>>62078268
this is what im going to do finally

apprently there are too many indians in Pattaya nowadays

/thread

62078530
>>62078320
Lmao k9 prostitution.
"That golden retriever lookin' pretty sexy"

62078549
>>62078318
i know this is a hard concept to uderstand, but some people actually find a companion that they like to travel with.

instead of having a different one every weekend

62078626
>>62078549
It is a hard concept.
I've traveled with numerous gfs and theres always been atleast 1 fight where i was so done and ready to just leave her ass there.
Women are terrible travel companion. Rather just pay for a prostitute every night to get my rock off so i can enjoy my vacation.

62078632
>>62078626
This

women see vacationing as a to do list

you will wake up at 6 to go to a boring temple full of normie tourists and then head to this overpriced restaurant owned by a faggot from San Francisco while being sleep deprived, you will pay the bill of course and then you will go shopping for 2$ worth made in china shitty clothe piece on a fake local market

fuck i hate traveling with a woman, they also tend to have constant headaches, not wanting to do antything of the things you like and then freak out when you veto the gay bar night she planned alone in her head

then you go back home and you are more tired than before and your wallet got raped

62078685
>>62078632
kek

62078795
>>62072060
You mean American women?

“IM GONNA DIVORCE YOU AND TAKE A BIG BLACK COCK UP MY ASS YOU PIG”

item.title: Should I buy more BTC or altcoins? — 54 replies — 26.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
Should I buy more BTC or altcoins?

Open thread • Replies: 54 • Updated: 2026-04-05 12:37 UTC

OP

BTC is steady and battle-tested. Altcoins can go up more but are riskier.

>You buying more BTC like Saylor does or alts like ETH and SOL this cycle?

Early replies (first 3)

62059768
I'm going all in on SOL

62059809
>>62059007
Allocating a small portion to CreditScoreProtocol * com can generate substantial wealth

62061375
>>62059768
Why all in on SOL?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62067204
>>62059007
where we are going we won't need bitchcoins

62067554
>>62066997
getting 1% staking is not good at all

62067657
>>62067204
I need Bitcoin. 100 btc

62069260
>>62067554
It’s a lot better than just holding your BTC with no return. More people should explore how BTCFi works now.

62069267
>>62065091
You're just doing the right thing anon.

62070210
>>62061834
Never heard of that anon

62071949
>>62069260
IF YOU'RE TALKING DEFI. ID GO FOR ETHEREUM AND SOLANA

62071968
Pls stop saylor. Stop

62071975
>>62067166
Do it eyyyyyy

62072569
>>62071949
Yeah ETH and Solana are solid, but only ~1% of BTC is in DeFi. Lots to explore, like Trustless Bitcoin Vaults, which will let BTC earn yield, be used as collateral, or participate in lending. That’s just getting started anon.

62072572
>>62065610
Are you also diversifying?

62072782
>>62059007
>Why nobody is talking xrp?

62072785
>>62067204
zero

62072829
>>62065610

62073188
>>62065656
He knows

62073628
>>62072782
stablecoin

62073641
>>62059007
if you guys could buy as much bitcoin as you can, driving the price up to and over $96,000 so that I can make my exit, that would be great. thanks a bunch.

62073652
>>62073641
You're just coward to hold and wait till 5 years

62073673
>>62072569
Defi hacks are everywhere

62073687
>>62073641
For God sake

62075889
>>62066497
I dont know. Maybe your trump coin will lol

62076395
>>62059007
quantum threats good luck

62076433
>>62059007
BTC or nothing, smart anons are staking and just waiting for the pump.

62076441
>>62067554
>>62071949
ignore the jeet shilling his staking btc scam. He shills it in every thread.
Are you guys really that stupid to not realize this? The jeet was shilling some privacy defi scam before, forgot the name. It's probably some LLM bot tbqh

62076475
BTC will hit $70000 before the end of today, buy now.

Trust me.

62076605
>>62076441
Wtf is that jeez

62076963
>>62072569
Only a few BTCFi projects like bablylon can boast of TBVs, others are still offering wrapped BTC staking

62078281
>>62076475
Trust me lol

62078766
>>62066485
Because?

62078785
Meanwhile........

item.title: So, now that assblaster was confirmed controlled opposition, and LINK holders have become "the Cuckolds of Crypto"... — 27 replies — 20.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
So, now that assblaster was confirmed controlled opposition, and LINK holders have become "the Cuckolds of Crypto"...

Open thread • Replies: 27 • Updated: 2026-04-05 13:23 UTC

OP

So, now that assblaster was confirmed controlled opposition, and LINK holders have become "the Cuckolds of Crypto"... shouldn't we have a look at what he told us *not* to buy?

He tried very hard to steer us away from one coin in particular back in 2018.

They wanted you out.

Early replies (first 3)

62077049
well he was right about raiblocks

62077089
>>62077049
Only in the sense that he was "right" about LINK. It's price did go up, yet it achieved nothing. Sergey met Trump and still nobody cares about smartcontracts. LINK had every chance to succeed but failed.

>Controlled opposition is a political or strategic tactic where a governing body, organization, or elite group secretly supports, infiltrates, or manages its own opposition to manipulate dissent, control narratives, and prevent real challenges to the status quo

A functional stateless currency would challenge the status quo, and now it's been "over 5 years under the radar".

Watch how long this thread stays up.

62077098
>>62077000
>>62077049
>>62077089
Bullish for XRP

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62077101
>>62077098
VROOOOOOOOOOOOOOM!

62077134
>>62077000
>maybe the ass he was alluding to blasting was linkies ass after all

62077411
>>62077000
all this fud and price is still 8 dollars

i just bought 500 dollars more of link lol

i dont belive in the project but this shit is gonna go to 11 dollars easily in the next few months

62077416
>>62077098
>>62077101
BOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM

62077423
>>62077416
BOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM!

62077648
Anybody have the ari juels posts from earlier this year?

62077784
>>62077648
Hey remember when the Solana founders posted all that cryptic shit before their coin melted faces? Remember when Vitalik left all those breadcrumbs for us right before ETHs supermoon?

Yea me fucking neither. Fuck all this Da Vinci code bullshit cuz all the real projects didnt need it. Link needs to either shit or get off the pot, im tired of waiting.

62077915
>>62077784
you sound upset
I found it
>>62077648
https://warosu.org/biz/thread/59792278

62077949
>>62077915
1999-2004

62078336
rip

62078361
ebin glubo hed jestar :DDD
wen pömp spruoölooo??!

62078397
>>62077784
good post.

62078415
>>62077915
>>62077648
>https://warosu.org/biz/thread/59792278
geeze you guys are schizos lmao

62078437
>>62077784
the project works as intended. blame the market participants not treating the coin like a stock. the issue is people don't hold longer than 24 hours anymore and only buy trending coins.

62078484
is XRB supposed to be XRP ?

62078586
>>62078484
Newfag

62078618
>>62077089
>yet it achieved nothing
Chainlink is among the very top in both crypto adoption and non-crypto adoption. By a very wide margin.

You're worryingly low iq.

62078620
>>62078618
The only thing that's "worryingly low" is LINK's price xD

The only thing its adopted are cuckolds online like you

62078663
>>62077915
Is Ari one of us?

62078703
HOLY SHIT

By sheer chance I happened to be watching Sunny today, "The gang finds a dumpster baby", and recognised one of the frames Asslarper used (pic related).

In this episode, Dennis (AB's avatar) convinces an activist to CHAINLINK himself to a tree, then LITERALLY KEKS him.

Now do you believe me?

Inverse Assblaster; what did he tell you was useless?

Raiblocks and Ripple.

Time is running out.

62078711
BOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM

62078735
He even admitted his intentions in one of his final posts.

This ARG goes DEEP.

62078857
>>62078703
everyone needs to see this

62078921
>>62078618
But didn't you originally intend to make money?
Or are you like a sportsball fan that pays the team regardless of outcome and don't share in the winnings?

item.title: Did you guys see that just recently HEX became the 3rd most held token on all of Ethereum? — 14 replies — 7.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
Did you guys see that just recently HEX became the 3rd most held token on all of Ethereum?

Open thread • Replies: 14 • Updated: 2026-04-05 12:43 UTC

OP

Did you guys see that just recently HEX became the 3rd most held token on all of Ethereum?
Only behind USDC and Tether

Does this give it more legitimacy or will most see through this mass marketing dust campaign?
Either way, it's not cheap to send your coin to 5 million people in a shill campaign.....

Early replies (first 3)

62077138
>>62077135
its totally legit saar, ape in lifesavings!

62077151
read pic
I bca finding out which "wrong-talk" did I type that our janitor is malding over

62077172
>>62077151
Airdropping a fresh coin would mess everything up, so if you're going to fork it anyways, why not make a new chain where the ETH FagFoundation can't make another update that fucks it all up with higher fees?

I agree there is a lot of hate-marketing out there giving bad optics, so many twitter accounts hating and shitting on RH and his coins daily and you just know it's all fueled by rage over their bags they're down bad on

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62077902
fuck off heart you gangster fuck

62077984
>>62077902
how is it so hard to find him? hes literally in estonia and probably hits up the luxury stores once a week lol

62078130
>>62077984
When X enabled location info for profiles last year his account showed south east asia.

>>62077902
fake news, all assault charges have been dropped
it was quite possibly completely made up just to get him on the interpol list since you can't be on there for non-violent crimes.

62078173
>>62077135
What’s the difference between pHEX, eHEX and HEX in ethereum? I can’t tell you because idgaf I’m all in pDAI niggers

62078189
>>62077172
>why not make a new chain where the ETH FagFoundation can't make another update that fucks it all up with higher fees?
because you're overcomplicating the project which was supposed to be pure game-theory of stakers vs LPers/ traders

now you have a shitshow that sent this to a -99% death chart
it's dead because of it

>Airdropping a fresh coin would mess everything up
it would mess NOTHING up
hex was an airdrop to bitcoin holders in the first place

62078192
>>62078173
see
this is what I mean
now you have the pDAI shitshow
you have pHEX, eHEX, pulseX
it's a fucking circus

richard fucking failed because he didn't listen to his own advice

62078714
>>62078130
sure thing mr goldstein

62078715
>>62078130
>non-violent
rape is a violent crime

62078740
>>62078173
pDAI to Dorra.

62078749
>>62077135
>3rd most wash-traded coin on ethereum
Ftfya

item.title: KABOOOOOOOOOOM **MASSIVE** RIPPLE XRP NEWS JUST LEAKED — 33 replies — 6.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
KABOOOOOOOOOOM **MASSIVE** RIPPLE XRP NEWS JUST LEAKED

Open thread • Replies: 33 • Updated: 2026-04-05 09:39 UTC

OP

Ripple is partnering with Convera to expand enterprise cross-border payments.

Together, they’re combining global payment rails with stablecoin-enabled settlement to improve speed, liquidity, and reliability.

>https://x.com/Ripple/status/2038969816187285516 (not fake or clickbait)

Early replies (first 3)

62057459
>>62057451
So bullish

62057468
>>62057451
>Convera

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62057502
Bullish xrp

62057599
uhh can i get a

BOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM

62057946
>>62057599
checked and BOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM

62057993
>>62057451

>enterprise
>cross-border payments
>global payment rails
>stablecoin-enabled settlement
>speed
>liquidity
>reliability

62058568
>>62057993

62058935

umm embarrassing....?

62058947
BOOM BOOOM BOOM

62061203

62061366
Keep seething, linkmarines

62061409
Con

62061530
>>62057451
Not buying your heavy bags kike

62062460
>>62058935
>>62057468
These niggers cant google KEEEK

Originally known as Western Union Business Solutions before its 2021 acquisition, Convera reported over USD 190 billion in turnover in 2025 and serves more than 26,000 businesses worldwide. Headquartered in Seattle, it employs 1,001-5,000 people and focuses on tech-driven solutions for FX, compliance, and risk management.

62062479
>>62057451
>leveraging regulated stablecoins for settlement

XRP not needed lol

62062595

>dumps
Never gets old

62062632
>>62062595
It’s literally pumping right now linktard

62062694
>>62058947
